interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y (12.68%) peaks above the 20Y (11.65%). The 91D-364D segment shows a dip at 364D (10.07% vs 182D 10.20%), suggesting near-term liquidity tightness. Yields above the 10% repo rate across all tenors reflect persistent inflationary expectations and tight banking sector liquidity. The BB's elevated repo rate anchors the short end, while the 2Y bulge signals market stress around medium-term inflation and fiscal borrowing pressures.
implications: The 2Y yield spike above longer tenors suggests banks are demanding a premium for medium-term government debt amid tight liquidity and heavy T-bond issuance by MoF
Persistent yields 100-250bps above the repo rate indicate BB's monetary tightening is transmitting but inflation expectations remain unanchored, limiting near-term rate cut prospects
The inverted 2Y-5Y segment may discourage bank participation in medium-tenor auctions, potentially pushing MoF toward shorter-duration borrowing and increasing rollover risk
